mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with month-over-month PAYEMS increases, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% restricts corporate borrowing and compresses valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when this threshold is breached.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the Sharpe ratio of the short signal is not significantly negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real retail sales and a declining federal funds rate indicate demand expansion amplified by monetary easing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and falling FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over main effects in an F-test with p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the unemployment rate is below 6%, reflecting capacity-constrained expansion.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6.0%, month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of instances, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts signals durable goods demand and construction sector momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST do not predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production to a new 12-month low signals negative earnings revisions and inventory destocking, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls to a new 12-month low
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when INDPRO hits a 12-month low, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly below zero in a one-sided t-test with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a low federal funds rate indicate negative real policy rates that stimulate borrowing and risk-taking, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: reflationary environment with policy restraint lag
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term fails to improve R-squared versus additive models in an F-test with p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating household income and falling corporate revenues, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the mean conditional return is not significantly negative.
